title: System and method for device interoperability and synchronization
abstract: What is disclosed is a device interoperability system for one or more user devices associated with a user, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a first user device, said device interoperability system comprising a communications module, wherein a first connection is established between said first user device and said communications module; storage associated with said device interoperability system and coupled to said communications module, wherein said storage stores an operating system, one or more programs, and data associated with the user, further wherein said operating system is booted by said first user device via said first connection; and one or more processors to support said device interoperability system.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A device interoperability system for one or more user devices associated with a user, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a first user device said device interoperability system comprising a communications module, wherein a first connection is established between said first user device and said communications module; storage coupled to said communications module, wherein said storage stores an operating system, one or more programs, and data associated with the user, further wherein said operating system is booted by said first user device via said first connection; and one or more processors to support said device interoperability system. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein said first connection is based on one of a direct wireless connection, a direct wired connection, and docking. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein said device interoperability system is installed on a gadget; and said device interoperability system comprises a battery to power said device interoperability system, and a charging module to charge said battery. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; and said device interoperability system is installed via integration into said second user device. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; and said device interoperability system is installed as an application on said second user device. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein said first user device operates in either an interoperability system mode or a stand-alone mode. 7. The system of claim 1, further wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; said operating system stores a first configuration set corresponding to the first user device, and a second configuration set corresponding to the second user device; and said operating system switches between said first configuration set and said second configuration set depending on whether said first user device or said second user device is used to boot said operating system. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein said first user device comprises a first user device storage; and said first user device storage is used for at least one of swapping, backing up data or caching. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein said operating system includes one or more kernels corresponding to one or more architectures. 10. The system of claim 1, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; a second connection is established between said second user device and said communications module; and said operating system is either shut down on said first user device before being booted by said second user device via said second connection; or paused on said first user device before being booted by said second user device via said second connection. 11. The system of claim 1, further comprising changing firmware of said first user device. 12. The system of claim 1, wherein said one or more user devices further comprises a set of user devices comprising one or more user devices different from said first device; a set of connections comprising one or more connections different from said first connection are established between said set of user devices and said communications module; and said operating system is booted by said set of user devices via said set of connections. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein said communications module simultaneously maintains said set of connections with said set of user devices; and each of a plurality of instances of said operating system runs on each of said set of user devices and said first user device. 14. The system of claim 1, wherein the operating system runs on the first user device; and the operating system uses processing capabilities of the first user device for operation. 15. The system of claim 1, wherein said one or more devices comprises a second user device with an integrated controller and an added external wireless adapter; said device interoperability system is installed on said second user device; and said communications module comprises the integrated controller and the added external wireless adapter. 16. A method for device interoperability for one or more user devices associated with a user, said one or more user devices comprising a first user device; said method comprising establishing a first connection between said first user device and a communications module within a device interoperability system; storing, using storage within the device interoperability system, an operating system, one or more programs, and data associated with the user; and 

 booting said operating system by said first user device via said first connection. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein said first connection is based on one of a direct wireless connection, a direct wired connection, and docking. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein said device interoperability system is installed on a gadget; and said device interoperability system comprises a battery to power said device interoperability system, and a charging module to charge said battery. 19. The method of claim 16, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; and said device interoperability system is installed via integration into said second user device. 20. The method of claim 16, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; and said device interoperability system is installed as an application on said second user device. 21. The method of claim 16, wherein said first user device operates in either an interoperability system mode or a stand-alone mode. 22. The method of claim 16, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; further wherein said method comprises storing, by said operating system, a first configuration set corresponding to the first user device, and a second configuration set corresponding to the second user device; and said operating system switching between said first configuration set and said second configuration set depending on whether said first user device or said second user device is used to boot said operating system. 23. The method of claim 16, wherein said first user device comprises a first user device storage; and said first user device storage is used for at least one of swapping, backing up data or caching. 24. The method of claim 16, wherein said operating system includes one or more kernels corresponding to one or more architectures. 25. The method of claim 16, wherein said one or more user devices comprises a second user device; said method further comprising establishing a second connection between said second user device and said communications module; and either shutting down said operating system on said first user device before said operating system is booted by said second user device via said second connection, or pausing said operating system on said first user device before said operating system is booted by said second user device via said second connection. 26. The method of claim 16, further comprising changing firmware of said first user device. 27. The method of claim 16, wherein said one or more user devices further comprises a set of user devices comprising one or more user devices different from said first device; said method comprising establishing a set of connections comprising one or more connections different from said first connection between said set of user devices and said communications module; and booting said operating system by said set of user devices via said set of connections. 28. The method of claim 27, further comprising maintaining, by said communications module, said set of connections simultaneously with said set of user devices; and each of a plurality of instances of said operating system runs on each of said set of user devices and said first user device. 29. The method of claim 16, wherein the operating system runs on the first user device; and the operating system uses processing capabilities of the first user device for operation. 30. The method of claim 16, wherein said one or more devices comprises a second user device with an integrated controller and an added external wireless adapter; said device interoperability system is installed on said second user device; and said communications module comprises the integrated controller and the added external wireless adapter.
